perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Payment Terms. Eutectix may issue an invoice for Liquidmetal Products any time after the shipment thereof to Liquidmetal. Payment terms are net 30 days after the date of the invoice, or the date of receipt by Liquidmetal of the invoice if the invoice is received more than 10 days after the date of the invoice. Unless otherwise stated in the applicable Order or as otherwise agreed in writing by the Parties, payment shall be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Payment Terms. Eutectix may issue an invoice for Liquidmetal Products any time after the shipment thereof to Liquidmetal. Payment terms are net 30 days after the date of the invoice, or the date of receipt by Liquidmetal of the invoice if the invoice is received more than 10 days after the date of the invoice. However, if Liquidmetal makes payment within 15 days of the invoice date, they are eligible for a 2% discount. Unless otherwise stated in the applicable Order or as otherwise agreed in writing by the Parties, payment shall be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by creating two different payment terms, where initially the payment terms where net 30 days, the change suggests that Liquidmetal gets 2% discount if they pays within 15 days creating contradictory terms. This is not mentioned or specified elsewhere in the document and undermines the previous payment term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Payment Terms. Eutectix may issue an invoice for Liquidmetal Products any time after the shipment thereof to Liquidmetal. Payment terms are net 30 days after the date of the invoice, or the date of receipt by Liquidmetal of the invoice if the invoice is received more than 10 days after the date of the invoice. However, if Liquidmetal makes payment within 15 days of the invoice date, they are eligible for a 2% discount. Unless otherwise stated in the applicable Order or as otherwise agreed in writing by the Parties, payment shall be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a discount for early payment, while the original text and contradicted text only specify payment terms of net 30 days. This creates a direct inconsistency in the payment terms, as Liquidmetal now has two options: pay within 15 days for a discount or pay within 30 days at full price, contradicting the initial net 30 day payment term.

perturbation.original_text: 5.4 Consequences of Termination. In the event this Agreement expires or is terminated for any reason other than a breach by Eutectix, Liquidmetal shall purchase from Eutectix existing raw material inventory at the purchase price (including delivery charges) paid by Eutectix to its suppliers in connection with the Orders accepted by Eutectix hereunder. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) Liquidmetal shall i. Pay Eutectix any amounts rightfully owing under each outstanding Order in accordance with the payment terms set forth in this Agreement; ii. Subject to Eutectix's right to finish manufacturing work in process as set forth below, have the unconditional right to possess or repossess the Licensed Equipment (as defined in Section 2.1) and take all actions it deems appropriate to effect such possession or repossession at its own cost; and (b) Eutectix shall immediately i. cease all activities under this Agreement (including exercising its rights under the licenses granted hereunder), unless and to the extent otherwise agreed or requested in writing by Liquidmetal; ii. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with regard to orders received and accepted by Eutectix before expiration or notice of termination, Eutectix may finish making any products in process, may conclude any orders in process, including finishing manufacturing of such products and shipping such products to the customer for up to 6 months after termination or expiration of this Agreement; iii. Comply with Section 9.2 and iv. transfer title and deliver to Liquidmetal, in the manner and to the extent requested in writing by Liquidmetal, such completed or partially completed Liquidmetal Products, drawings and other information Eutectix has produced or acquired in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.4 Consequences of Termination. In the event this Agreement expires or is terminated for any reason other than a breach by Eutectix, Liquidmetal shall purchase from Eutectix existing raw material inventory at the purchase price (including delivery charges) paid by Eutectix to its suppliers in connection with the Orders accepted by Eutectix hereunder. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) Liquidmetal shall i. Pay Eutectix any amounts rightfully owing under each outstanding Order in accordance with the payment terms set forth in this Agreement; ii. Subject to Eutectix's right to finish manufacturing work in process as set forth below, have the unconditional right to possess or repossess the Licensed Equipment (as defined in Section 2.1) and take all actions it deems appropriate to effect such possession or repossession at its own cost; and (b) Eutectix shall immediately i. cease all activities under this Agreement (including exercising its rights under the licenses granted hereunder), unless and to the extent otherwise agreed or requested in writing by Liquidmetal; ii. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with regard to orders received and accepted by Eutectix before expiration or notice of termination, Eutectix may finish making any products in process, may conclude any orders in process, including finishing manufacturing of such products and shipping such products to the customer for up to 3 months after termination or expiration of this Agreement; iii. Comply with Section 9.2 and iv. transfer title and deliver to Liquidmetal, in the manner and to the extent requested in writing by Liquidmetal, such completed or partially completed Liquidmetal Products, drawings and other information Eutectix has produced or acquired in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by creating two different timings, where initially Eutectix had 6 months to finish any products in process, the change suggests that Eutectix only has 3 months to finish the order. This contradicts other parts of the contract that specify longer timeframes, undermining the previous completion date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Consequences of Termination. In the event this Agreement expires or is terminated for any reason other than a breach by Eutectix, Liquidmetal shall purchase from Eutectix existing raw material inventory at the purchase price (including delivery charges) paid by Eutectix to its suppliers in connection with the Orders accepted by Eutectix hereunder. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) Liquidmetal shall i. Pay Eutectix any amounts rightfully owing under each outstanding Order in accordance with the payment terms set forth in this Agreement; ii. Subject to Eutectix's right to finish manufacturing work in process as set forth below, have the unconditional right to possess or repossess the Licensed Equipment (as defined in Section 2.1) and take all actions it deems appropriate to effect such possession or repossession at its own cost; and (b) Eutectix shall immediately i. cease all activities under this Agreement (including exercising its rights under the licenses granted hereunder), unless and to the extent otherwise agreed or requested in writing by Liquidmetal; ii. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with regard to orders received and accepted by Eutectix before expiration or notice of termination, Eutectix may finish making any products in process, may conclude any orders in process, including finishing manufacturing of such products and shipping such products to the customer for up to 3 months after termination or expiration of this Agreement; iii. Comply with Section 9.2 and iv. transfer title and deliver to Liquidmetal, in the manner and to the extent requested in writing by Liquidmetal, such completed or partially completed Liquidmetal Products, drawings and other information Eutectix has produced or acquired in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5.4(b)(ii) of the agreement, altering the timeframe Eutectix has to finish manufacturing products post-termination from 6 months to 3 months. The contradicted text is the same section as the changed text, creating a direct inconsistency within the contract regarding the allowable completion timeframe. This inconsistency introduces a strong in-text contradiction because it directly conflicts, undermining the initial agreed-upon completion date, thus satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 14.6 Definition of "Affiliate". For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Affiliate" means, with respect to any specified person or entity, any corporation, limited liability company or other legal entity which directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with specified person or entity or its successors or assigns. For the purposes of this Agreement, "control" shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding shares on a fully diluted basis or other voting rights of the specified entity to elect directors or managers, or the right to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of the specified entity whether by contract or otherwise; and the terms "controlling" and "controlled" have meaning correlative to the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.6 Definition of "Affiliate". For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Affiliate" means, with respect to any specified person or entity, any corporation, limited liability company or other legal entity which directly or indirectly works in conjunction with, under a partnership with, or strategic allience with specified person or entity or its successors or assigns. For the purposes of this Agreement, "control" shall mean the direct or indirect agreement between both parties. In the event, one party does not feel like, the allience or partnership is working for them, they reserve the right to cease alligations with the other party; and the terms "controlling" and "controlled" have meaning correlative to the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of "Affiliate" to include strategic partnerships and loosely affiliated entities, rather than just entities under majority control. This contradicts other contract sections that rely on the original definition (50% ownership) to determine rights and obligations. Additionally, allowing case-by-case determination undermines the original intent of treating affiliates the same as contracting parties, introducing legal uncertainty and potential disputes over obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.8 Royalties for Transactions Not at Arm's Length. Eutectix agrees that in the event any Licensed Products shall be sold (1) to any Affiliate (as defined herein), or (2) to a corporation, firm, or association with which, or individual with whom Eutectix or its stockholders or Affiliates shall have any agreement, understanding, or arrangement (such as, among other things, an option to purchase stock, or an arrangement involving a division of profits or special rebates or allowances) without which agreement, understanding, or arrangement, prices paid by such a corporation, firm, association or individual for the Licensed Products would be higher than the Net Sales Price reported by Eutectix, or if such agreement, understanding, or arrangement results in extending to such corporation, firm, association or individual lower prices for Licensed Products than those charged to outside concerns buying similar products in similar amounts and under similar conditions, then, and in any such events, the royalties to be paid hereunder in respect of such Licensed Products shall be computed based on an assumed or deemed Net Sales Price equal to those charged to such outside concerns.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Affiliate" in Section 14.6 broadens the term to include entities with strategic alliances or partnerships, moving away from the original definition focused on ownership and control. This directly contradicts Section 2.8, which calculates royalties for transactions with "Affiliates (as defined herein)" based on an arm's length principle. The original definition allows a clear determination of which entities fall under the affiliate umbrella, enabling straightforward royalty calculation. The modified definition, by contrast, introduces ambiguity and subjectivity. The new definition means the application of royalty calculations becomes uncertain, as a "strategic alliance" is open to interpretation, directly breaking compatibility with Section 2.8's intent and creating a strong in-text contradiction.
